concerns.text: T032 introduces 'cardinality-normalized Excess Overlap' as a deliverable. The spec (FR-004, SC-001) strictly mandates Jaccard and Dice indices measured against a baseline of perfect overlap (Jaccard=1.0). Introducing a new metric without explicit spec authorization constitutes a silent relaxation of the defined success criteria and a drift from the constitutional requirement to measure against the specified baseline.

T031 specifies a fallback to 500 iterations if time > 6h. The spec (FR-006) mandates a minimum of 1,000 iterations. While the edge case section mentions a 6h limit, the functional requirement explicitly sets 1,000 as the floor. A task that allows the deliverable to fall below 1,000 iterations without a prior spec amendment weakens the FR-006 constraint.

T024 implements spatial mapping in `code/overlap.py` under User Story 2. FR-005 (US3) explicitly requires this mapping to be a prerequisite for Spearman correlation. Placing the implementation in US2 creates a logical gap where the US3 task (T030) cannot be verified as compliant with FR-005's 'ONLY after mapping' constraint unless the dependency is strictly enforced in the execution plan, which is not reflected in the task grouping.
